summary: Draft PR claimed to match `hsd_80394314` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c`, but the only diff changed the storage declaration for `hsd_804CF810` in `particle.static.h` by removing `static`. The author closed it after recognizing a title/body-vs-diff symbol mismatch; decomp-dev's no-progress/no-changes verdict for the labeled symbol was correct. The useful takeaway is process-oriented: automated PR generation needs a pre-open soundness gate to verify that the labeled target symbol, diff, and objdiff progress actually agree before consuming reviewer time.
what_changed: The only code change was in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.static.h`.

Declaration/comment block around particle globals changed `/* 4CF810 */ static struct ParticleScreenState hsd_804CF810;` to `/* 4CF810 */ struct ParticleScreenState hsd_804CF810;`, removing internal linkage from `hsd_804CF810`.
No diff was shown for `src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c`, despite the PR body claiming candidate source `particle.c` line 1961 and symbol `hsd_80394314`.
The PR was closed unmerged because the diff touched `hsd_804CF810`, not the labeled target `hsd_80394314`.
smart_moves: The author explicitly closed the draft once the mismatch was identified instead of trying to reinterpret it as a valid `hsd_80394314` match.
The postmortem comment names the failure mode clearly: title-vs-diff symbol mismatch, body template leaks, and zero-progress objdiff.
A pre-open soundness gate was reportedly added to the local HERMES runner to catch mismatched target symbols and zero-progress diffs before opening future PRs.
decomp_lessons: For decomp PRs, exact-match verification metadata is not enough if the generated patch does not actually modify or improve the labeled symbol.
Before opening an automated match PR, cross-check the PR title, body target symbol, changed declarations, and objdiff result against the same symbol.
Header-only storage-class changes can affect relocation/linkage behavior, but they must be tied to the intended function or object; otherwise they risk being noise or misleading progress.
A decomp-dev or objdiff 'No changes' verdict for the labeled symbol should be treated as a blocker when the PR claims a match.
naming_conventions: Static/global particle variables in `particle.static.h` use address-based names such as `hsd_804CF7E8`, `hsd_804CF810`, and `hsd_804D0908` with address comments like `/* 4CF810 */`.
Particle arrays in this area use descriptive camelCase-ish names such as `psFormGroupArray`, `psTexGroupArray`, `psCmdListArray`, and `psNumTexGroup`.
The PR title followed the common `Match <symbol>` convention, but this PR demonstrates that the convention is only useful if validated against the actual diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The claimed verification was an accepted exact match with verifier exit code 0, but the evidence was undermined by a mismatch between claimed symbol `hsd_80394314` and changed symbol `hsd_804CF810`.

The body mentions the candidate reason as a `.bss.0 relocation symbol` issue, and the actual patch removed `static` from a BSS-like global declaration, but the PR comment confirms this was not valid progress for the labeled symbol.
Future automation should include an objdiff zero-progress check and symbol-to-diff consistency check before submitting generated patches.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present.
Author comment: closing as draft because HERMES labeled it for `hsd_80394314` but the diff only touched `hsd_804CF810`.
Author acknowledged decomp-dev bot's 'No changes' verdict against the labeled symbol was correct.
Author reported adding a pre-open soundness gate for title-vs-diff symbol mismatch, body template leaks, and zero-progress objdiff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that actually match `hsd_80394314`.
Search for subsequent valid changes to `hsd_804CF810` or `ParticleScreenState` linkage in `particle.static.h`.
Look for commits or PRs mentioning the HERMES pre-open soundness gate for title-vs-diff mismatch and zero-progress objdiff.
Audit other automated `Match <symbol>` PRs around May 2026 for body template leaks or mismatched changed symbols.
